  "findings": [
    "Flashing is not lapped or sealed at joints",
    "Inadequate moisture barrier continuity behind masonry veneer"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Lap flashing joints a minimum of 6 inches and seal with compatible adhesive/mastic",
    "Ensure flashing is fully integrated with the water-resistive barrier before masonry veneer is closed in"
  ],
  "rationale": "Flashing must be continuous, lapped, and sealed to prevent water penetration into the building envelope. Unsealed flashing joints will allow water to bypass the drainage plane, risking structural rot and mold.",
  "references": [
    "TMS 402",
    "BIA Technical Notes 7",
    "IRC Section R703.8"
  ]
